lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਹਸਾ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਨੈਣੀ ਲਾਗਸਿ ਬਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: antar sahasaa baahar maaeaa nainee laagas baanee |
lines.english: There is doubt within me, and Maya is outside; it hits me in the eyes like an arrow.
Wherever I look, there I see Him pervading. ||3||
Raamkalee, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਦਰਿ ਵਸਹਿ ਕਵਨੁ ਦਰੁ ਕਹੀਐ ਦਰਾ ਭੀਤਰਿ ਦਰੁ ਕਵਨੁ ਲਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: jit dar vaseh kavan dar kaheeai daraa bheetar dar kavan lahai |
lines.english: Where is that door, where You live, O Lord? What is that door called? Among all doors, who can find that door?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਣਵਤਿ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਦਾਸਨਿ ਦਾਸਾ ਪਰਤਾਪਹਿਗਾ ਪ੍ਰਾਣੀ ॥੪॥੨॥
lines.translit: pranavat naanak daasan daasaa parataapahigaa praanee |4|2|
lines.english: Prays Nanak, the slave of the Lord's slaves: such a mortal suffers terribly. ||4||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਦਰ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਫਿਰਾ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਸੋ ਦਰੁ ਕੋਈ ਆਇ ਕਹੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jis dar kaaran firaa udaasee so dar koee aae kahai |1|
lines.english: For the sake of that door, I wander around sadly, detached from the world; if only someone would come and tell me about that door. ||1||
How can I cross over the world-ocean?

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਖੁ ਦਰਵਾਜਾ ਰੋਹੁ ਰਖਵਾਲਾ ਆਸਾ ਅੰਦੇਸਾ ਦੁਇ ਪਟ ਜੜੇ ॥
lines.translit: dukh daravaajaa rohu rakhavaalaa aasaa andesaa due patt jarre |
lines.english: Pain is the door, and anger is the guard; hope and anxiety are the two shutters.
While I am living, I cannot be dead. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਇਆ ਜਲੁ ਖਾਈ ਪਾਣੀ ਘਰੁ ਬਾਧਿਆ ਸਤ ਕੈ ਆਸਣਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਰਹੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: maaeaa jal khaaee paanee ghar baadhiaa sat kai aasan purakh rahai |2|
lines.english: Maya is the water in the moat; in the middle of this moat, he has built his home. The Primal Lord sits in the Seat of Truth. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਊਚਾ ਨਹੀ ਕਹਣਾ ਮਨ ਮਹਿ ਰਹਣਾ ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਆਪਿ ਕਰੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: aoochaa nahee kahanaa man meh rahanaa aape jaanai aap kare |3|
lines.english: Do not speak out loud - remain in your mind. The Lord Himself knows, and He Himself acts. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿੰਤੇ ਨਾਮਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਿਆ ਤੁਮ ਸਰਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਅਵਰੁ ਹਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: kinte naamaa ant na jaaniaa tum sar naahee avar hare |
lines.english: You have so many Names, Lord, I do not know their limit. There is no other equal to You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਆਸਾ ਅੰਦੇਸਾ ਤਬ ਹੀ ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਏਕੁ ਕਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: jab aasaa andesaa tab hee kiau kar ek kahai |
lines.english: As long as there is hope, there is anxiety; so how can anyone speak of the One Lord?

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਭੀਤਰਿ ਰਹੈ ਨਿਰਾਸਾ ਤਉ ਨਾਨਕ ਏਕੁ ਮਿਲੈ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: aasaa bheetar rahai niraasaa tau naanak ek milai |4|
lines.english: In the midst of hope, remain untouched by hope; then, O Nanak, you shall meet the One Lord. ||4||
This is the way to remain dead while yet alive. ||1||Second Pause||3||
In this way, you shall cross over the world-ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਤੁ ਝੋਲੀ ਮੰਗਣ ਕੈ ਤਾਈ ਭੀਖਿਆ ਨਾਮੁ ਪੜੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: pat jholee mangan kai taaee bheekhiaa naam parre |1|
lines.english: Honor is my begging-bowl, and the Naam, the Name of the Lord, is the charity I receive. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਸਾਖੀ ਮੇਰੀ ਸਿੰਙੀ ਬਾਜੈ ਲੋਕੁ ਸੁਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: surat sabad saakhee meree singee baajai lok sune |
lines.english: Awareness of the Shabad and the Teachings is my horn; the people hear the sound of its vibrations.
Raamkalee, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੋਰਖੁ ਸੋ ਜਿਨਿ ਗੋਇ ਉਠਾਲੀ ਕਰਤੇ ਬਾਰ ਨ ਲਾਗੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gorakh so jin goe utthaalee karate baar na laagai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He alone is Gorakh, who sustains the earth; He created it in an instant. ||1||Pause||
O Baba, Gorakh is the Lord of the Universe; He is always awake and aware.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਰਣ ਜੀਵਣ ਕਉ ਧਰਤੀ ਦੀਨੀ ਏਤੇ ਗੁਣ ਵਿਸਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: maran jeevan kau dharatee deenee ete gun visare |2|
lines.english: To die and to live, He gave us the earth, but we have forgotten these blessings. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਣੀ ਪ੍ਰਾਣ ਪਵਣਿ ਬੰਧਿ ਰਾਖੇ ਚੰਦੁ ਸੂਰਜੁ ਮੁਖਿ ਦੀਏ ॥
lines.translit: paanee praan pavan bandh raakhe chand sooraj mukh dee |
lines.english: Binding together water and air, He infused the breath of life into the body, and made the lamps of the sun and the moon.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਧ ਸਾਧਿਕ ਅਰੁ ਜੋਗੀ ਜੰਗਮ ਪੀਰ ਪੁਰਸ ਬਹੁਤੇਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: sidh saadhik ar jogee jangam peer puras bahutere |
lines.english: There are so many Siddhas, seekers, Yogis, wandering pilgrims, spiritual teachers and good people.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਤਿਨ ਮਿਲਾ ਤ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਆਖਾ ਤਾ ਮਨੁ ਸੇਵ ਕਰੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: je tin milaa ta keerat aakhaa taa man sev kare |3|
lines.english: If I meet them, I chant the Lord's Praises, and then, my mind serves Him. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਗਦੁ ਲੂਣੁ ਰਹੈ ਘ੍ਰਿਤ ਸੰਗੇ ਪਾਣੀ ਕਮਲੁ ਰਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: kaagad loon rahai ghrit sange paanee kamal rahai |
lines.english: Paper and salt, protected by ghee, remain untouched by water, as the lotus remains unaffected in water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਸੇ ਭਗਤ ਮਿਲਹਿ ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਤਿਨ ਜਮੁ ਕਿਆ ਕਰੈ ॥੪॥੪॥
lines.translit: aise bhagat mileh jan naanak tin jam kiaa karai |4|4|
lines.english: Those who meet with such devotees, O servant Nanak - what can death do to them? ||4||4||
Raamkalee, First Mehl:
Listen, Machhindra, to what Nanak says.
He alone is a hermit, who attains such understanding.
One who practices Yoga in such a way,
One who subdues the five passions does not waver.
saves himself, and saves all his generations. ||1||
He begs for loving devotion to the Lord, and lives in the Fear of God.
Day and night, he remains absorbed in deepest Samaadhi. ||1||Pause||
He is satisfied, with the priceless gift of contentment.
Becoming the embodiment of meditation, he attains the true Yogic posture.
He focuses his consciousness in the deep trance of the True Name. ||2||
Listen, O Machhindra: this is the insignia of the true hermit.
Nanak chants the Ambrosial Bani.
One who, in the midst of hope, remains untouched by hope,
shall truly find the Creator Lord. ||3||
The Guru and His disciple are joined together!
One who eats this food, this medicine of the Teachings,
Prays Nanak, I share the mysterious secrets of God.
